payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Child claims completed tasks for compensation
payload.justification: Signal SG915 describes the strategic process of children claiming tasks they have completed.

payload.description: Parent defines compensated tasks and monetary values
payload.justification: Signal SG916 articulates the parental process of defining what tasks exist and their associated compensation.

payload.description: Children view available task opportunities
payload.justification: Signal SG917 describes the process of providing visibility into available opportunities for children.

payload.description: Children view completed task accomplishments
payload.justification: Signal SG917 describes the distinct process of viewing what has been completed, separate from viewing opportunities.

payload.description: Parent reviews earnings and task completion for distribution
payload.justification: Signal SG919 establishes the parental oversight process of reviewing completion and earnings before compensation distribution.

payload.description: Task available for completion with associated monetary compensation
payload.justification: Signal SG915 describes the core purpose involving tasks that children complete for compensation, indicating Task as a strategically important entity. | Signals SG915 and SG916 both reference monetary compensation and values tied to tasks, establishing compensation as a distinct strategic entity.

payload.description: Weekly earnings accumulation for children
payload.justification: Signals SG918 and SG919 reference earnings accumulation and review, indicating earnings as a strategically important tracked entity.

payload.description: Parent-child task-based compensation system where parent defines tasks with associated monetary values and child receives payment upon task completion
payload.justification: Signal SG915 establishes the strategic relationship between task completion action and resulting compensation. | Signal SG916 describes the relationship between parent actor and task/value definition at strategic level.

payload.description: Weekly earnings and task completion cycle
payload.justification: Signals SG915 and SG918 both reference weekly timeframes for task completion and earnings accumulation, indicating a strategic cycle.

payload.description: Child user claiming and completing tasks
payload.justification: Signals SG915, SG917, and SG918 consistently reference children as strategic actors performing task completion, claiming, and viewing actions.

payload.description: Parent user defining tasks and overseeing compensation
payload.justification: Signals SG916 and SG919 identify parents as strategic actors with task definition and oversight responsibilities.

payload.description: Enable children to earn monetary compensation through task completion
payload.justification: Signal SG915 establishes the core strategic goal of the enterprise system.

payload.description: Empower parents to control task definition and compensation structure
payload.justification: Signal SG916 articulates the parental motivation as a strategic goal of the system.

payload.description: Provide children transparency into opportunities, achievements, and earnings progress
payload.justification: Signal SG917 expresses the strategic goal of visibility and transparency for child users. | Signal SG918 defines the strategic purpose of earnings awareness and tracking.

payload.description: Enable parental oversight and verification before compensation distribution
payload.justification: Signal SG919 establishes the strategic motivation for parental review and control mechanisms.

payload.description: Parent defines tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: SG922 describes the parent capability to define tasks as a business function.

payload.description: Child views available and completed tasks within weekly scope
payload.justification: SG924 defines child visibility requirements as a business function.

payload.description: Child views weekly earnings total at period end
payload.justification: SG925 establishes earnings visibility as a business function performed by children.

payload.description: Parent views child-task-earnings relationships for payment reconciliation
payload.justification: SG926 defines parent reporting capability as a business function supporting payment reconciliation.

payload.description: Task availability state resets on weekly cycle
payload.justification: SG927 establishes a business rule governing task state transitions.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained beyond current cycle
payload.justification: SG928 requires retention of historical data as a business rule.

payload.description: Task with associated monetary value
payload.justification: SG922 establishes tasks as business entities with monetary attributes.

payload.description: Child
payload.justification: SG923 and SG924 reference child as a distinct business entity that completes tasks and views information.

payload.description: Task completion assignment to child
payload.justification: SG923 specifies the conceptual relationship linking completed tasks to child entities.

payload.description: Task availability and completion status
payload.justification: SG924 references available tasks and SG927 describes resetting task availability state, indicating this as a business attribute. | SG924 distinguishes between available and completed tasks, indicating completion status as an attribute.

payload.description: Child earnings aggregated by weekly period
payload.justification: SG925 establishes weekly aggregation of earnings as a conceptual attribute visible to children.

payload.description: Weekly business cycle
payload.justification: SG924, SG925, and SG927 all reference weekly scope and cycles as the primary business time period.

payload.description: Weekly period end
payload.justification: SG925 references period end as a distinct business event when earnings visibility occurs.

payload.description: Parent role
payload.justification: SG922 and SG926 describe parent-specific capabilities, establishing parent as a distinct business role.

payload.description: Child role
payload.justification: SG924 and SG925 describe child-specific visibility and capabilities, establishing child as a distinct business role.

payload.description: Track child pocket money earnings through task completion
payload.justification: SG921 explicitly defines the core business purpose of the system.

payload.description: Task visibility scoped to weekly period
payload.justification: SG924 establishes weekly scope as a constraint on task visibility.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Participation Roles
payload.description: Defines who may participate in the household economic learning system and what fundamental relationship structure governs their interactions, establishing parent authority and child participation boundaries.

payload.name: Task-Based Compensation Obligation
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise commitment to define work opportunities with associated monetary value and fulfill compensation upon verified completion, governing what the household promises and delivers.

payload.name: Parental Oversight Authority
payload.description: Governs the enterprise requirement for parental verification, review, and approval control over work claims and compensation distribution, ensuring accountability before economic transfer occurs.

payload.name: Child Economic Transparency Entitlement
payload.description: Ensures children have visibility into available opportunities, completed accomplishments, and earnings status, establishing the enterprise obligation to maintain transparent access to economic information.

payload.name: Work Claim Processing
payload.description: Defines the enterprise responsibility to receive, record, and process claims of completed work from child participants, establishing the mechanism through which compensation obligations are triggered.

payload.name: Weekly Settlement Cycle
payload.description: Establishes the temporal rhythm governing when compensation accumulation is assessed, reviewed, and distributed, providing predictable economic cycle boundaries for all participants.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining compensable work obligations, establishing their monetary worth, maintaining their lifecycle states, and governing their availability across temporal boundaries.

payload.name: Earnings Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing child entitlement to compensation through work obligation fulfillment, recording completion assignments, and maintaining transparent earnings aggregation across business cycles.

payload.name: Settlement Oversight
payload.description: Business responsibility for validating the integrity of work-compensation relationships to enable accurate payment reconciliation and fulfillment of financial obligations to participants.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define tasks with associated monetary values.
payload.rationale: Parent authority to establish tasks with monetary value is fundamental to the task-based compensation system structure

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim tasks.
payload.rationale: Child participation through task claiming is a core capability of the household economic learning system

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to complete tasks.
payload.rationale: Task completion by children is essential to triggering the compensation mechanism

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide payment to children upon task completion.
payload.rationale: Payment upon completion closes the task-based compensation cycle and delivers the economic learning outcome

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish parent authority over task definition.
payload.rationale: Parent-child authority relationship over task definition is a governance constraint defining participation boundaries within the household economic learning system

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish parent authority over compensation oversight.
payload.rationale: Parent-child authority relationship over compensation oversight is a governance constraint defining participation boundaries within the household economic learning system

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define tasks and establish monetary compensation values.
payload.rationale: Parent control over task definition and compensation structure is a core functional commitment derived from the How-column process CCI.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to earn monetary compensation through task completion.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to provide the capability for children to complete tasks and receive compensation, derived from the primary Why-column goal.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall empower parents to control task definition.
payload.rationale: Governance constraint establishing parent authority over task definition, derived from the Why-column goal asserting parental control as a policy commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall empower parents to control compensation structure.
payload.rationale: Governance constraint establishing parent authority over the compensation framework, derived from the Why-column goal asserting parental control as a policy commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish parental oversight over task completion.
payload.rationale: Addresses the enterprise commitment to enable parents to review and verify that children have completed tasks. Derives from the How-column process obligation (parent reviews task completion) and the Why-column goal (enable parental oversight).

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish parental oversight over compensation distribution.
payload.rationale: Addresses the enterprise commitment to enable parents to review and verify earnings before compensation is distributed. Derives from the How-column process obligation (parent reviews earnings) and the Why-column goal (enable parental verification before distribution).

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define tasks and govern compensation.
payload.rationale: Derived from the Who-column actor CCI describing the parent role. At Row 1, this expresses the enterprise commitment to enable the parent actor's definitional and governance authority over work and compensation within the household economic system.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view available tasks.
payload.rationale: Row 1 enterprise commitment to provide children visibility into available task opportunities. Process CCI from How column expressing capability to view tasks in their available state.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to view completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Row 1 enterprise commitment to provide children visibility into completed task accomplishments. Process CCI from How column expressing capability to view tasks in their completed state.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children transparency into available tasks and completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Row 1 governance constraint from Why column establishing the enterprise's commitment to task transparency as a policy objective. Covers visibility into both task opportunities and achievements.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide children transparency into earnings.
payload.rationale: Row 1 governance constraint from Why column establishing the enterprise's commitment to economic transparency regarding accumulated rewards.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim completed tasks for compensation.
payload.rationale: Both CCIs converge on a single enterprise obligation: to provide the capability for child participants to claim completed work and thereby trigger compensation. CCI-ROW1-C-How-001 describes the process (claiming for compensation), and CCI-ROW1-C-Who-001 identifies the actor (child). This is one enterprise commitment at the scope level, not two separate obligations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly cycle for task completion.
payload.rationale: The weekly cycle boundary governs the temporal rhythm of task completion assessment, providing predictable periodicity for task evaluation. This is a constraint on the temporal organization of task-related enterprise operations.
payload.fit_criteria: The cycle period shall be seven consecutive days.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly cycle for earnings accumulation.
payload.rationale: The weekly cycle boundary governs the temporal rhythm of earnings accumulation, providing predictable economic periodicity. This is a constraint on the temporal organization of earnings-related enterprise operations.
payload.fit_criteria: The cycle period shall be seven consecutive days.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish tasks available for completion with associated monetary compensation.
payload.rationale: What-column Entity CCI asserting the existence and composition of the domain entity 'task' with its structural attributes (availability for completion, association with monetary compensation). At Row 1 enterprise scope, this is the commitment to establish the entity and its defining characteristics.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks with monetary values definable by parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance complementing the parent's action, stating what capability the system provides at the boundary without prescribing implementation.

payload.statement: A task shall have an associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: Structural requirement derived from the task entity, expressing the compositional obligation that a task carries a monetary attribute.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks claimable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance enabling the child's claim action, providing the boundary capability without specifying implementation.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks completable by children.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance enabling the child's completion action, providing the boundary capability without specifying implementation.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of task completion assignments to children.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to record the relationship between completed tasks and children, enabling downstream payment obligations.

payload.statement: The business shall provide payment to children for completed tasks.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for fulfilling financial obligations to children based on work completion, a behind-the-boundary settlement function.

payload.statement: The business shall restrict task definition authority to parents.
payload.rationale: Governance constraint establishing that only parents may define tasks, elaborating the enterprise-level authority commitment as a business rule.

payload.statement: The business shall restrict compensation oversight authority to parents.
payload.rationale: Governance constraint establishing that only parents may oversee compensation, elaborating the enterprise-level authority commitment as a business rule.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of child earnings aggregated by weekly period.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to maintain aggregated earnings data per child per cycle, enabling transparent compensation tracking.

payload.statement: The business shall restrict monetary value assignment authority to parents.
payload.rationale: Governance constraint establishing that only parents may set compensation values, elaborating the enterprise-level control commitment as a business rule.

payload.statement: The system shall make task completion assignments visible to parents.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance enabling parental oversight of completion status, providing the boundary capability without specifying implementation.

payload.statement: The system shall make child earnings visible to parents for reconciliation.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance enabling parental oversight of compensation amounts, providing the boundary capability without specifying implementation.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks visible to children within weekly scope.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing children access to current-cycle tasks, bounded by the weekly business cycle constraint.

payload.statement: The business shall scope task visibility to the weekly period.
payload.rationale: Temporal governance constraint restricting task visibility to the current weekly cycle, elaborating the boundary of what children may see.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed tasks visible to children within weekly scope.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing children access to their completion history, bounded by the weekly business cycle constraint.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce a weekly reset cycle for task availability state.
payload.rationale: Temporal governance constraint establishing the business rule that task availability resets weekly, bounding the child's view of available work.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task data beyond the current weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to preserve task history across cycle boundaries, enabling completed task visibility and audit.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks visible to entitled children within the current weekly period.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance: what the system provides to enable task visibility, constrained by weekly scope.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed tasks visible to entitled children within the current weekly period.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance: what the system provides to enable completed task visibility, constrained by weekly scope.

payload.statement: The system shall make weekly earnings totals visible to entitled children.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance: what the system provides to enable earnings transparency.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain child earnings aggregated by weekly period.
payload.rationale: Business-subject artefact responsibility: maintaining the earnings aggregation that enables transparency.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed tasks claimable by entitled children.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance: what the system provides to enable task claiming.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain task completion assignments to children.
payload.rationale: Business-subject artefact responsibility: maintaining the completion-assignment relationship that records claims.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce task availability state resets on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Business-subject governance rule: the temporal constraint on task lifecycle state managed behind the boundary.
payload.fit_criteria: Task availability states reset at the start of each weekly period.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain task availability status.
payload.rationale: Business-subject artefact responsibility: maintaining the availability state attribute that enables weekly reset enforcement.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain task completion status.
payload.rationale: Business-subject artefact responsibility: maintaining the completion state attribute that enables weekly reset enforcement.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce earnings accumulation within weekly period boundaries.
payload.rationale: Business-subject governance rule: the temporal constraint on earnings aggregation aligned to the business cycle.
payload.fit_criteria: Child earnings are aggregated and bounded by the weekly period in which tasks are completed.

payload.statement: The system shall enable parents to define tasks with associated monetary values.
payload.rationale: System-subject black-box affordance: what the system provides to enable task definition with compensation.

payload.statement: The Task conceptual entity shall define: attributes: task_id [PK] identifier (ref), monetary_value quantity (ref), availability_status lifecycle_state (new), completion_status lifecycle_state (new).
payload.rationale: Task entity with monetary value and lifecycle states (availability, completion) expressed in What-column CCIs.

payload.statement: The system shall make tasks definable by parents with monetary values.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance enabling parent task definition capability.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce task availability state reset on the weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Business rule governing temporal lifecycle transitions aligned to weekly business cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task records beyond the current weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility for record stewardship across temporal boundaries.

payload.statement: The business shall constrain task visibility to the current weekly period.
payload.rationale: Why-column governance constraint scoping task visibility by temporal boundary.

payload.statement: The Child conceptual entity shall define: attributes: child_id [PK] identifier (ref).
payload.rationale: Child entity is the core participant in the earnings accountability domain; identity refinement from Row 1 scope-level child participant concept.

payload.statement: The TaskCompletionAssignment conceptual entity shall realise the relationship: attributes: child_id [FK] identifier →Child (real), task_id [FK] identifier →Task (real), completion_status lifecycle_state (new); relationships to: Child (many-to-one), Task (many-to-one).
payload.rationale: Task completion assignment realises the relationship between Child and Task at the business level, establishing completion as the basis for earnings entitlement.

payload.statement: The ChildEarnings conceptual entity shall introduce: attributes: child_id [FK] identifier →Child (new), weekly_period temporal_partition (new), earnings_total quantity (new); relationships to: Child (many-to-one).
payload.rationale: Child earnings is the business artefact that aggregates compensation entitlement across weekly periods, establishing transparent accountability for pocket money through task completion.

payload.statement: The system shall make available tasks visible to the entitled child within the current weekly period.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing visibility of available tasks to entitled children; black-box provision at the boundary scoped to the current weekly period.

payload.statement: The system shall make completed tasks visible to the entitled child within the current weekly period.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance providing visibility of completed tasks to entitled children; black-box provision at the boundary scoped to the current weekly period.

payload.statement: The system shall make the weekly earnings total visible to the entitled child at the end of each weekly period.
payload.rationale: System-subject affordance that complements the child's earnings-viewing capability; black-box provision of aggregated earnings visibility triggered by period-end event.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of task completion assignments to children establishing entitlement to compensation.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility behind the boundary; maintaining the record of completion assignments is the mechanism by which earnings accountability is established.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain child earnings aggregated by weekly period.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to maintain the earnings artefact partitioned by weekly period, supporting transparent pocket money tracking.

payload.statement: The TaskCompletion conceptual entity shall realise the relationship: attributes: task_id [FK] identifier →Task (real), child_id [FK] identifier →Child (real), completion_timestamp event_time (new); relationships to: Task (many-to-one), Child (many-to-one).
payload.rationale: The task completion assignment relationship between Task and Child is a first-class entity at the business level, tracking which child completed which task for settlement purposes.

payload.statement: The ChildEarnings conceptual entity shall introduce: attributes: child_id [FK] identifier →Child (new), weekly_period_id [FK] time_period_identifier →WeeklyPeriod (new), total_earnings quantity (new); relationships to: Child (many-to-one), WeeklyPeriod (many-to-one).
payload.rationale: Child earnings aggregated by weekly period represents a business-level settlement artefact required for payment reconciliation.

payload.statement: The system shall make child-task-earnings relationships visible to the parent for payment reconciliation.
payload.rationale: The system-subject affordance that enables the parent's viewing capability. This is the black-box capability the system provides at the boundary (WHAT it offers, not HOW it is implemented).

payload.statement: The business shall aggregate child earnings by weekly period.
payload.rationale: The business responsibility (behind the boundary) to aggregate earnings at weekly period boundaries for settlement integrity and reconciliation. The When-column CCI (weekly period end) provides the temporal context for this aggregation obligation.

payload.statement: A child can view their own earnings balance and transaction history.
payload.rationale: Row 2 actor-subject statement (boundary-crossing Who-column). The child role interacts with the system to access their earnings information. Uses actor-subject form per guidance (a) — the child is the subject performing the real action (view), not buried in an object. This is a capability the system affords at the boundary to the child actor.

payload.statement: A parent can define tasks with associated monetary compensation values.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject elaboration of the parent's capability to define compensable work. Voices the Who-column boundary-crossing action and the What-column task entity with monetary value.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a task with its associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: Structural obligation for the task entity and its monetary attribute, derived from the What-column entity CCI. Required to realize the parent's definition capability.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce that only parents may define tasks and establish their monetary values.
payload.rationale: Constraint elaboration enforcing parental exclusivity over task definition authority. Voices the Why-column control obligation implied by 'empower parents to control'.

payload.statement: A parent can define tasks with monetary compensation values.
payload.rationale: Actor-subject elaboration of the parent's task definition capability at the business boundary, satisfying the 'define tasks' aspect of the seed.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain task availability status and completion status.
payload.rationale: Structural obligation for task lifecycle states required to realize governance of compensable work across temporal boundaries.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce the weekly reset cycle for task availability state.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain historical task data beyond the current weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: Business record-keeping responsibility supporting compensation governance across time. How-column business capability distinct from the reset-cycle constraint.

payload.description: Establishes the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed tasks for monetary compensation throughout the week.
Articulates the parental motivation to define compensated tasks and associated monetary values.
Expresses the goal of providing children visibility into available opportunities and their completed accomplishments.
Defines the purpose of enabling children to understand their weekly earnings accumulation.
Establishes parental oversight motivation to review earnings and task completion for compensation distribution.
Section heading indicating conceptual system description follows.
Defines the core business purpose as tracking child pocket money earnings through task completion.
Establishes parent capability to define tasks as business entities with associated monetary values.
Specifies the conceptual relationship linking completed tasks to child entities.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for available and completed task entities within weekly scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation concept for child earnings visibility at period end.
Defines parent reporting capability showing child-task-earnings relationships for payment reconciliation.
Establishes weekly cycle business rule resetting task availability state.
Requires retention of historical task entity data beyond current cycle.
